title: betula pubescens var. carpatica
intro: Betula pubescens var. carpatica, also known as the Carpathian birch or European white birch, is a subspecies of the Betula pubescens tree, native to the Carpathian Mountains in Eastern Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has been used to treat inflammation, fever, and pain. The health benefits of Betula pubescens var. carpatica include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antimicrobial agent, making it useful in treating various infections and wounds. The herbal preparations of Betula pubescens var. carpatica include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
